title: End-to-End RAG Architecture, Versioning, Caches, and Failures · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved trace contains a plausible versioned end-to-end RAG service with one ingestion, normalization, chunking, lexical, embedding, index, filter, fusion, reranking, context, generation, citation, authorization, latency, or recovery defect; identify the first failed stage before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in versioned end-to-end RAG service and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Define typed inputs, outputs, identities, limits, errors, telemetry, and ownership for ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, and evaluation stages.
Bind each run to corpus, document, chunk, parser, embedding, index, query, reranker, prompt, generator, policy, and evaluation versions.
Design embedding, retrieval, reranking, context, and answer cache keys and invalidation from every behavior-changing input and authorization boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug RAG engineering team handles case 3014 during a multimodal maintenance-manual release. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose versioned ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, and cache contracts; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. steps.title: RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Streaming Response Ownership
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-streaming-response-ownership-5dbd25" that uses case 9647 to compose versioned ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, and cache contracts. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Streaming Response Ownership.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Streaming Response Ownership into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Streaming Response Ownership, keep Rag Streaming Response Ownership connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-streaming-response-ownership-5dbd25
# Competency: rag-streaming-response-ownership.
# Case 9647: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation.
# Changed case: change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence.
def evidence_rag_streaming_response_ownership_5dbd25(corpus_version: int = 12, embedding_version: int = 7, index_version: int = 12, prompt_version: int = 9, stage_count: int = 6, idempotent: bool = True, cache_key_complete: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_5dbd25 = "5dbd25-52dbd5-9647"
    assert evidence_variant_5dbd25
    evidence_axes_5dbd25 = (
        "corpus5dbd25",
        "query52dbd5",
        "tenant9647",
        "index5dbd259647",
        "prompt52dbd59647",
        "evaluation96475dbd25",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_5dbd25)) == 6
    if min(corpus_version, embedding_version, index_version, prompt_version) < 1:
        return False, 0, "version-contract"
    if index_version != corpus_version:
        return False, stage_count, "index-corpus-skew"
    if stage_count < 5:
        return False, stage_count, "missing-stage"
    if not idempotent or not cache_key_complete:
        return False, stage_count, "replay-cache"
    identity = corpus_version + embedding_version + prompt_version
    assert identity > index_version
    return True, identity, "versioned-pipeline"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
